tools.name: get_audience
tools.description: Get an audience by its ID, including its filter definition.

tools.name: list_audience_members
tools.description: List all members of an audience.

tools.name: list_audiences
tools.description: List all audiences in the workspace.

tools.name: update_audience
tools.description: Create or update an audience with a filter definition.

tools.name: delete_audience
tools.description: Delete an audience by its ID.

tools.name: get_audit_event
tools.description: Get a specific audit event by its ID.

tools.name: list_audit_events
tools.description: List audit events in the workspace. Useful for tracking API usage and changes.

tools.name: generate_jwt_for_user
tools.description: Generate a JWT authentication token for a user. Used for client-side SDK auth (Inbox, Preferences, etc.).

tools.name: invoke_automation_template
tools.description: Invoke an automation run from an existing automation template. Call list_automations first to get the template_id. Example: { template_id: "auto-onboarding", recipient: "user-123", data: { plan: "pro" } }.

tools.name: invoke_ad_hoc_automation
tools.description: Invoke an ad-hoc automation with inline steps. Valid step actions: send, send-list, delay, cancel, update-profile, invoke, fetch-data. To cancel a previously started automation, use the cancel_automation tool instead.

tools.name: list_automations
tools.description: List automation templates in the workspace. Always call this first to discover template_id values before calling invoke_automation_template. Optionally filter by version.

tools.name: cancel_automation
tools.description: Cancel a running automation by its cancelation_token. This invokes a second ad-hoc automation with a single cancel step. The token must match the cancelation_token set when the original automation was started. Note: spelling is "cancelation_token" (single "l").

tools.name: create_brand
tools.description: Create a new brand. The API requires settings — omitting it returns a 400. If you do not have specific brand colors, omit settings and a safe default will be used automatically (black primary, white secondary). Example: { name: "Acme", settings: { colors: { primary: "#1a73e8", secondary: "#ffffff" }

tools.name: get_brand
tools.description: Get a brand by its ID.

tools.name: list_brands
tools.description: List all brands in the workspace.

tools.name: update_brand
tools.description: Replace an existing brand with new values.

tools.name: delete_brand
tools.description: Delete a brand by its ID.

tools.name: create_bulk_job
tools.description: Create a new bulk job for sending messages to multiple recipients. Workflow: create_bulk_job → add_bulk_users → run_bulk_job.

tools.name: add_bulk_users
tools.description: Add users to an existing bulk job.

tools.name: run_bulk_job
tools.description: Run a bulk job, triggering delivery to all added users.

tools.name: get_bulk_job
tools.description: Get the status of a bulk job.

tools.name: list_bulk_users
tools.description: List the users in a bulk job.

tools.name: courier_installation_guide
tools.description: Get the Courier SDK installation guide for a specific platform. For client-side SDKs (React, iOS, Android, Flutter, React Native), also generates a sample JWT.

tools.name: track_inbound_event
tools.description: Track an inbound event that can trigger automations. Requires event name, messageId (for deduplication), and properties.

tools.name: list_lists
tools.description: Get all lists. Optionally filter by pattern (e.g. 'example.list.*').

tools.name: get_list
tools.description: Get a list by its ID.

tools.name: get_list_subscribers
tools.description: Get all subscribers of a list.

tools.name: create_list
tools.description: Create or update a list by list ID.

tools.name: subscribe_user_to_list
tools.description: Subscribe a user to a list. Creates the list if it doesn't exist.

tools.name: unsubscribe_user_from_list
tools.description: Unsubscribe a user from a list.

tools.name: delete_list
tools.description: Delete a list by its ID.

tools.name: restore_list
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted list.

tools.name: bulk_subscribe_to_list
tools.description: Replace all subscribers on a list with the given recipients.

tools.name: add_subscribers_to_list
tools.description: Append subscribers to a list without removing existing subscribers.

tools.name: list_messages
tools.description: List messages you've previously sent. Filter by status, recipient, notification, provider, tags, or tenant.

tools.name: get_message
tools.description: Get the full details and status of a single message by its ID.

tools.name: get_message_content
tools.description: Get the rendered content (HTML, text, subject) of a previously sent message.

tools.name: get_message_history
tools.description: Get the event history for a message, showing each step in the delivery pipeline (enqueued, sent, delivered, etc.).

tools.name: cancel_message
tools.description: Cancel a message that is currently being delivered. Returns the message details with updated status.

tools.name: resend_message
tools.description: Resend a previously sent message. Loads the original send request and enqueues a brand-new send to the same recipient with the same content, producing a new messageId; the original message is unchanged. Rate limited per message (429 on rapid repeats).

tools.name: list_notifications
tools.description: List notification templates. Optionally filter by cursor.

tools.name: get_notification_content
tools.description: Get the published content blocks of a notification template.

tools.name: get_notification_draft_content
tools.description: Get the draft (unpublished) content blocks of a notification template.

tools.name: create_notification
tools.description: Create a V2 notification template. name is required. Provide content inline or set it immediately after creation via put_notification_content. To send with this template you must publish it first via publish_notification (or pass state: 'PUBLISHED' on create). Link a routing strategy via notificatio

tools.name: get_notification
tools.description: Retrieve a notification template by ID. Optionally request draft, published, or a version such as v001.

tools.name: replace_notification
tools.description: Replace a notification template entirely (full document PUT).

tools.name: archive_notification
tools.description: Archive a notification template by ID.

tools.name: list_notification_versions
tools.description: List version history for a notification template.

tools.name: publish_notification
tools.description: Publish a notification template, making it available for sending. Must be called before send_message_template unless the template was created with state: 'PUBLISHED'. Publishes the current draft by default; pass version (e.g. 'v001') to publish a specific historical version. Returns 204 on success.

tools.name: list_notification_checks
tools.description: List checks for a notification submission.

tools.name: update_notification_checks
tools.description: Update check statuses for a notification submission.

tools.name: put_notification_content
tools.description: Replace the elemental content of a V2 notification template. Overwrites all elements. Use channel elements to target specific channels. Multi-channel example: elements: [{ type: "channel", channel: "email", elements: [{ type: "meta", title: "Hello" }, { type: "text", content: "Email body" }] }, { ty

tools.name: put_notification_element
tools.description: Update a single element within a V2 notification template.

tools.name: put_notification_locale
tools.description: Set locale-specific content overrides for a V2 notification template. Each element override must reference an existing element by its id. Example for Spanish locale: { notification_id: "nt_01abc", locale_id: "es", elements: [{ id: "elem_meta_1", title: "Restablecer contraseña" }, { id: "elem_text_1"

tools.name: cancel_notification_submission
tools.description: Cancel a notification template submission.

tools.name: get_user_profile_by_id
tools.description: Get a user profile by their ID. Returns profile data including email, phone, and custom properties.

tools.name: create_or_merge_user
tools.description: Create a new user profile or merge supplied values into an existing profile (POST). Existing fields not included are preserved.

tools.name: replace_profile
tools.description: Fully replace a user profile (PUT). All existing data is overwritten; include every field you want to keep.

tools.name: patch_profile
tools.description: Partially update a user profile via JSON Patch (RFC 6902). Use add/replace/remove operations on specific profile paths.

tools.name: delete_profile
tools.description: Delete a user profile permanently.

tools.name: get_user_list_subscriptions
tools.description: Get all list subscriptions for a user.

tools.name: subscribe_user_to_lists
tools.description: Subscribe a user to one or more lists. Creates lists that do not exist.

tools.name: delete_user_list_subscriptions
tools.description: Delete all list subscriptions for a user.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a message to a user using inline title and body content (no template). Optionally specify routing channels.

tools.name: send_message_template
tools.description: Send a message to a user using a published notification template. The template must be published before sending — call publish_notification first if needed. Example: { user_id: "user-123", template: "nt_01abc123", data: { name: "Alex", resetUrl: "https://app.example.com/reset" } }.

tools.name: send_message_to_list
tools.description: Send a message to all subscribers of a list using inline title and body content.

tools.name: send_message_to_list_template
tools.description: Send a message to all subscribers of a list using a notification template.

tools.name: get_tenant
tools.description: Get a tenant by its ID.

tools.name: create_or_update_tenant
tools.description: Create or replace a tenant. Tenants represent organizations or groups that users belong to.

tools.name: list_tenants
tools.description: List all tenants in the workspace.

tools.name: delete_tenant
tools.description: Delete a tenant by its ID.

tools.name: list_tenant_users
tools.description: List users associated with a tenant.

tools.name: update_tenant_preference
tools.description: Set the default notification preference for a subscription topic on a tenant. This controls tenant-level defaults — it does NOT set per-user preferences (use the user preferences API for that). The topic_id must already exist as a subscription topic in the workspace; a 404 means the topic has not be

tools.name: delete_tenant_preference
tools.description: Remove default notification preference for a topic from a tenant.

tools.name: list_tenant_templates
tools.description: List notification templates configured for a tenant.

tools.name: get_tenant_template
tools.description: Get a tenant notification template association by template ID.

tools.name: replace_tenant_template
tools.description: Create or replace a tenant notification template (draft unless published is true).

tools.name: publish_tenant_template
tools.description: Publish a version of a tenant notification template.

tools.name: get_tenant_template_version
tools.description: Get a specific version of a tenant notification template (e.g. latest, published, or v1).

tools.name: delete_tenant_template
tools.description: Delete a tenant notification template. Returns 204 on success, 404 if the template does not exist for this tenant.

tools.name: get_translation
tools.description: Get a translation for a specific locale (e.g. "en_US", "fr_FR").

tools.name: update_translation
tools.description: Create or update a translation for a specific locale.

tools.name: list_user_push_tokens
tools.description: List all push/device tokens for a user.

tools.name: get_user_push_token
tools.description: Get a specific push/device token for a user.

tools.name: create_or_replace_user_push_token
tools.description: Create or replace a push/device token for a user.

tools.name: bulk_add_user_tokens
tools.description: Add multiple push/device tokens for a user in one request. Overwrites matching existing tokens.

tools.name: patch_user_token
tools.description: Apply a JSON Patch (RFC 6902) to a specific push token.

tools.name: delete_user_token
tools.description: Delete a specific push token for a user.

tools.name: get_user_preferences
tools.description: Get a user's notification preferences (subscriptions, opt-outs, channel preferences).

tools.name: get_user_preference_topic
tools.description: Get a user's preference for a specific subscription topic.

tools.name: update_user_preference_topic
tools.description: Update a user's preference for a specific subscription topic (opt in, opt out, or set channel preferences).

tools.name: delete_user_preference_topic
tools.description: Delete a user's preference for a specific subscription topic, reverting it to the topic's default status.

tools.name: list_user_tenants
tools.description: List all tenants a user belongs to.

tools.name: add_user_to_tenant
tools.description: Add a user to a tenant.

tools.name: remove_user_from_tenant
tools.description: Remove a user from a tenant.

tools.name: bulk_add_user_tenants
tools.description: Add a user to multiple tenants at once. A custom profile can be supplied per tenant.

tools.name: remove_all_user_tenants
tools.description: Remove a user from all tenants.

tools.name: bulk_update_user_preferences
tools.description: Additively create or update a user's preferences for one or more topics in a single request. Only the topics in the body are touched; existing overrides for other topics are left untouched. Partial-success: valid topics are written and returned in `items`, unapplicable ones collected in `errors`.

tools.name: bulk_replace_user_preferences
tools.description: Replace a user's complete set of preference overrides in one request. The topics in the body become the recipient's entire override set: listed topics are created or updated, and every existing override not included is reset to its topic default. An empty `topics` array clears all overrides. Validat

tools.name: archive_request
tools.description: Archive a send request and all its associated messages by request ID.

tools.name: create_routing_strategy
tools.description: Create a routing strategy defining how notifications are delivered across channels and providers.

tools.name: get_routing_strategy
tools.description: Retrieve a routing strategy by ID. Returns the full entity including routing, channels, and providers.

tools.name: replace_routing_strategy
tools.description: Replace a routing strategy. Full document replacement; missing optional fields are cleared.

tools.name: archive_routing_strategy
tools.description: Archive a routing strategy. The strategy must not have associated notification templates; unlink all templates before archiving.

tools.name: list_routing_strategies
tools.description: List routing strategies in the workspace. Returns metadata only; use get for full details.

tools.name: list_routing_strategy_notifications
tools.description: List notification templates associated with a routing strategy. Useful for checking linked templates before archiving.

tools.name: list_journeys
tools.description: List journey templates in the workspace. Call this first to discover journey IDs before calling invoke_journey, get_journey, or replace_journey. Optionally filter by version (published or draft).

tools.name: invoke_journey
tools.description: Invoke a journey run from a journey template. Call list_journeys first to find the template_id. Example: { template_id: "j-onboarding", user_id: "user-123", data: { plan: "pro" } }.

tools.name: create_journey
tools.description: Create a new journey. Defaults to DRAFT state. Send nodes are not allowed on create — create the shell with a trigger node, then call replace_journey to add send nodes after linking notification templates. Call publish_journey to make it live. Node ids are server-generated; do NOT include an id fiel

tools.name: get_journey
tools.description: Get a journey by ID. Pass version=draft to retrieve the working draft, or version=vN for a historical version. Defaults to published.

tools.name: replace_journey
tools.description: Replace (update) a journey draft. Full document replacement — include all nodes and properties in the body. Call publish_journey afterwards to make changes live, or pass state: "PUBLISHED" to publish immediately. Send node template IDs must already be scoped to this journey.

tools.name: publish_journey
tools.description: Publish the current draft of a journey, making it live and invokable. Pass version to roll back to a prior published version instead of publishing the draft. Returns 404 if there is no draft to publish.

tools.name: archive_journey
tools.description: Archive a journey. Archived journeys cannot be invoked but existing runs continue to completion.

tools.name: list_journey_versions
tools.description: List published versions of a journey, ordered most recent first.

tools.name: list_journey_templates
tools.description: List notification templates scoped to a journey. Journey-scoped templates can only be used by send nodes within the same journey. Call this to discover template IDs before wiring send nodes in replace_journey.

tools.name: create_journey_template
tools.description: Create a notification template scoped to a journey. Defaults to DRAFT; pass state: "PUBLISHED" to publish on create. The template can then be referenced in journey send nodes. Example: { journey_id: "j-abc", channel: "email", notification: { name: "Welcome Email", tags: [], brand: null, subscription

tools.name: get_journey_template
tools.description: Get a journey-scoped notification template by notification ID. Pass version=draft to retrieve the working draft (required before the template has been published). Defaults to published.

tools.name: replace_journey_template
tools.description: Replace the draft of a journey-scoped notification template. Full document replacement. Call publish_journey_template afterwards to make it live.

tools.name: archive_journey_template
tools.description: Archive a journey-scoped notification template. Archived templates cannot be sent.

tools.name: publish_journey_template
tools.description: Publish the current draft of a journey-scoped notification template. Optionally pass version to roll back to a prior version.

tools.name: list_journey_template_versions
tools.description: List published versions of a journey-scoped notification template, ordered most recent first.

tools.name: get_journey_template_content
tools.description: Fetch the elemental content of a journey-scoped notification template. Pass version=draft for the working draft, or vN for a historical version. Defaults to published.

tools.name: put_journey_template_content
tools.description: Replace the elemental content of a journey-scoped notification template. Overwrites all elements. Call publish_journey_template afterwards to make it live.

tools.name: put_journey_template_locale
tools.description: Set locale-specific content overrides for a journey-scoped notification template. Each element override must reference an existing element by its id.

tools.name: cancel_journey
tools.description: Cancel journey runs. Supply EXACTLY ONE of cancelation_token (cancels every run associated with the token) or run_id (cancels a single run). Cancelation is idempotent: a run that already finished or was already canceled is left unchanged.

tools.name: list_providers
tools.description: List configured provider integrations for the workspace.

tools.name: get_provider
tools.description: Fetch a single provider configuration by ID.

tools.name: list_provider_catalog
tools.description: List available provider types from the catalog with their configuration schemas.

tools.name: create_provider
tools.description: Create a new provider (integration) configuration. Once routing strategies or notification templates reference this config, credential or settings mistakes can affect live sends—confirm provider key and settings against list_provider_catalog before saving. The provider field must be a known Courier

tools.name: update_provider
tools.description: Replace an existing provider configuration. Full replacement — retrieve current config with get_provider first; omitted optional fields are cleared. Changing API keys or settings affects live delivery if this integration is in use.

tools.name: delete_provider
tools.description: Delete a provider configuration. Returns 409 if the provider is still referenced by routing or notifications.

tools.name: list_preference_sections
tools.description: List the workspace's preference sections. Each section embeds its topics.

tools.name: create_preference_section
tools.description: Create a preference section in your workspace. The section id is generated and returned. Add topics afterwards with create_preference_topic.

tools.name: get_preference_section
tools.description: Retrieve a preference section by id, including its topics.

tools.name: replace_preference_section
tools.description: Replace a preference section. Full document replacement; missing optional fields are cleared. Topics attached to the section are unaffected.

tools.name: archive_preference_section
tools.description: Archive a preference section. The section must be empty: delete its topics first, otherwise the request fails with 409.

tools.name: publish_preferences
tools.description: Publish the workspace's preferences page. Takes a snapshot of every section with its topics under a new published version, making the current state visible on the hosted preferences page.

tools.name: list_preference_topics
tools.description: List the topics in a preference section.

tools.name: create_preference_topic
tools.description: Create a subscription preference topic inside a section. The topic id is generated and returned. Fails with 404 if the section does not exist.

tools.name: get_preference_topic
tools.description: Retrieve a topic within a section. Returns 404 if the section or topic does not exist, or the topic belongs to a different section.

tools.name: replace_preference_topic
tools.description: Replace a topic within a section. Full document replacement; missing optional fields are cleared.

tools.name: archive_preference_topic
tools.description: Archive a topic within a section.

tools.name: release_digest
tools.description: Release a digest schedule early — send what users have collected so far now instead of waiting for the scheduled time. A 204 is also returned when the schedule has no in-progress instances to release.

tools.name: list_digest_instances
tools.description: List the digest instances for a schedule. Each instance represents the events accumulated for a single user against the schedule, useful for monitoring accumulation before a digest is released.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_automations" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List automation templates in the workspace. Always call this first to discover template_id values before calling invoke_automation_template. Optionally filter b…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_journeys" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List journey templates in the workspace. Call this first to discover journey IDs before calling invoke_journey, get_journey, or replace_journey. Optionally filt…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.courier.com" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_messages, get_message, get_message_content, get_message_history, list_notifications, get_notification_content, get_notification_draft_content, get_notification, list_notification_versions, list_notification_checks, update_notification_checks, get_user_profile_by_id, send_message_to_list, send_message_to_list_template, list_tenant_templates, get_tenant_template, get_tenant_template_version, get_user_preferences, list_routing_strategy_notifications, list_journey_templates, get_journey_template, list_journey_template_versions, get_journey_template_content · Private data: generate_jwt_for_user, cancel_automation, replace_notification, list_user_push_tokens, get_user_push_token, create_or_replace_user_push_token, bulk_add_user_tokens, patch_user_token, delete_user_token, replace_routing_strategy, replace_journey, replace_journey_template, cancel_journey, create_provider, update_provider, replace_preference_section, replace_preference_topic · Public sink: invoke_ad_hoc_automation, resend_message, create_notification, publish_notification, put_notification_content, get_user_profile_by_id, create_or_merge_user, send_message, send_message_template, send_message_to_list, send_message_to_list_template, update_tenant_preference, publish_tenant_template, archive_request, create_journey, replace_journey, publish_journey, list_journey_templates, create_journey_template, publish_journey_template, publish_preferences, release_digest · Destructive: delete_audience, invoke_automation_template, delete_brand, run_bulk_job, delete_list, patch_profile, delete_profile, delete_user_list_subscriptions, send_message_template, delete_tenant, delete_tenant_preference, delete_tenant_template, delete_user_token, delete_user_preference_topic, remove_user_from_tenant, remove_all_user_tenants, bulk_replace_user_preferences, invoke_journey, cancel_journey, delete_provider, archive_preference_section.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_messages, get_message, get_message_content, get_message_history, list_notifications, get_notification_content, get_notification_draft_content, get_notification, list_notification_versions, list_notification_checks, update_notification_checks, get_user_profile_by_id, send_message_to_list, send_message_to_list_template, list_tenant_templates, get_tenant_template, get_tenant_template_version, get_user_preferences, list_routing_strategy_notifications, list_journey_templates, get_journey_template, list_journey_template_versions, get_journey_template_content · Private data: generate_jwt_for_user, cancel_automation, replace_notification, list_user_push_tokens, get_user_push_token, create_or_replace_user_push_token, bulk_add_user_tokens, patch_user_token, delete_user_token, replace_routing_strategy, replace_journey, replace_journey_template, cancel_journey, create_provider, update_provider, replace_preference_section, replace_preference_topic · Public sink: invoke_ad_hoc_automation, resend_message, create_notification, publish_notification, put_notification_content, get_user_profile_by_id, create_or_merge_user, send_message, send_message_template, send_message_to_list, send_message_to_list_template, update_tenant_preference, publish_tenant_template, archive_request, create_journey, replace_journey, publish_journey, list_journey_templates, create_journey_template, publish_journey_template, publish_preferences, release_digest · Destructive: delete_audience, invoke_automation_template, delete_brand, run_bulk_job, delete_list, patch_profile, delete_profile, delete_user_list_subscriptions, send_message_template, delete_tenant, delete_tenant_preference, delete_tenant_template, delete_user_token, delete_user_preference_topic, remove_user_from_tenant, remove_all_user_tenants, bulk_replace_user_preferences, invoke_journey, cancel_journey, delete_provider, archive_preference_section

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
